data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-24T00:21 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-07qybh [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-23 · Updated: 2026-02-23

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-bcnin5: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-be8r6i: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-k7parg: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-w5fdbp: Check for orphaned processes ● P2

content.thinking:
All children of this patrol wisp are marked as complete (✓). This patrol cycle is done. I should squash this wisp and start a new patrol cycle.

Let me check state.json first to see patrol count and whether there was extraordinary action.

content.text: All steps in this patrol wisp are already complete. Let me check state and squash.
Patrol count is 16, no extraordinary action. Under the 20-loop threshold. Squashing completed wisp and starting a new cycle.

input.command: gt mol squash --jitter 10s --summary="Patrol complete: all steps already closed from prior session"

content.thinking:
The Dolt database is crashing with a nil pointer dereference. This is the same error seen in the startup context. The wisp-based beads seem to be having Dolt issues. Let me try using `gt` commands instead to work with the patrol, or try a different approach.

Let me check if the wisp was actually created by looking at the hook.

content.text: Dolt database crash on `bd show`. This is a recurring SIGSEGV in the embedded Dolt driver. Let me check the hook and try to work with `gt` commands instead.

content.text: The new wisp was created (`skogtown-wisp-wisp-lwkv1a`) but it's not on the hook and `bd show` crashes with Dolt SIGSEGV. Let me try to hook it and work around the crash.
